title: fix: snap flux-2-dev dimensions to supported api.airforce sizes
author: pollinations-ai[bot]

gist.headline: Getting Flux into shape
gist.blurb: Fixed a bug where flux-2-dev was stubbornly only generating square images. Now it correctly snaps to beautiful landscapes and portraits!
gist.summary: Fixed an issue where the flux-2-dev image model ignored width and height parameters by snapping requested dimensions to the closest supported aspect ratio.
gist.impact: Users generating images with flux-2-dev will now correctly receive landscape and portrait aspect ratios instead of always getting 1024x1024 squares.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee and the Monitor Robot adjusting the size of a picture frame or canvas from a square to a rectangle, representing the fix for aspect ratios.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot wearing a tiny painter's beret, stretching a square canvas into a wide landscape rectangle. A chunky pixel robot with a CRT monitor head stands nearby holding a wooden ruler. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Emotionally warm, nostalgic but beautiful.
